DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: JONATHAN U. LEE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, AND REQUESTS FOR DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND FULLY AND PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,850, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, AND REQUESTS FOR DOCUMENTS FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: GREGORY M. HAYNES, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, VERLIE TATUM. JONATHAN U. LEE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MOTION ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORY, AND VERIFICATIONS IS MOOT AS IT SEEK VERIFICATIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS A FURTHER RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1. THE RESPONSE MUST BE MADE THAT IS COMPLETE AT THE TIME IT IS MADE. WHILE LATER OBTAINED DISCOVERY MAY CHANGE CERTAIN RESPONSES, THOSE CHANGES MAY BE VOLUNTEERED OR MAY BE OBTAINED BY SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORIES. IT IS NOT A PROPER OBJECTION TO STATE THAT RESPONSES CANNOT BE MADE WITHOUT PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 1, LIMITED TO THE PERIOD FIVE YEARS PRIOR TO THE INCIDENT TO PRESENT. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. MODIFICATION: RESPOND WITHIN 20 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

GREGORY M. HAYNES, ESQ., (415) 546-0777, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, VERLIE TATUM

RULING:
MATTER ARGUED. MR. HAYNES INTERPOSED A MOTION FOR A CONTINUANCE OF HEARING PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 437(c)h. THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. Each side is to submit an order in compliance with CCP SECTION 437(c)g for the Court's consideration. ARGUMENT EXCEEDED AN HOUR; Fees are imposed pursuant to GC 68086ff and CRC 892. (301/JLW/DS) Reporter: CHRISTINA PAXTON CSR# 1558
